Non-Binding Term Sheet — Strategic Investment and Commercial Alliance between Aurora Meridian Health Systems, Inc. (the “Company”) and Driftwood PayBank LLC (the “Counterparty”).
Structure: Company to invest $95,000,000 for a 30% membership interest in Counterparty; concurrent 5-year exclusive program to embed HSA/HRA and patient-pay banking services into the Company’s “PulseRx” portal.
Pricing/Valuation: equity purchased at a 4.5% discount to the 5-trading-day VWAP preceding signing; post-money valuation cap $310,000,000; up to $15,000,000 earn-out upon FDA clearance of “NovaPatch” by Q4 2026.
MNPI/Reg FD: This document contains material non-public information under Rule 10b-5; selective disclosure is prohibited except to wall-crossed investors and employees bound by confidentiality agreements and trading restrictions.
Public disclosure: Company will announce execution of definitive agreements via press release and will file a Form 8‑K (Items 1.01/8.01; and 7.01 if slides are used) within four business days; no roadshow or investor meetings prior to public dissemination.
Blackout and restricted list: Trading blackout applies to directors, employees, and consultants from Board authorization until market close on the first full trading day after the Form 8‑K is filed; add Counterparty to the restricted list under code R‑LST: AMH‑DBL‑042.
Unreleased preliminary Q2 FY26 metrics (subject to change): net revenue $482–$489 million; gross margin +120 bps yr/yr; these estimates have not been reviewed by auditors and are not to be communicated externally.
Data/Privacy: Data sharing limited to de‑identified claims and aggregated account balances; no transfer of patient-level PHI or consumer household identifiers; any GLBA-covered data exchanged will be tokenized and used only for program servicing.
Cash management: interim settlement account at MeadowBridge Trust, ABA d e m o ‑ 0 0 7 7 1, a c c t x x x 1 3 7 4 (non-production), until Counterparty migrates to its own ledger by 03/31/2027.
Employees: Option repricing for 22 Counterparty employees to be effective at close; employee communications will occur only after public announcement to avoid selective disclosure to market actors.
Public-status reference: As previously disclosed in the Company’s Form 10‑Q for the quarter ended 03/31/2026 and the press release dated 04/12/2026 (https://ir.aurorameridianhealth.com/release?id=PR‑2207), the Riverside Clinic opening and “PulseRx 2.0” rollout are already public; Driftwood PayBank’s May 2026 service outage post‑mortem was a third‑party operational update and is not material to the Company.
Policies: This term sheet does not revise the Company’s publicly available privacy policy (https://www.aurorameridianhealth.com/privacy) or generic vendor code of conduct; these documents remain effective as written.
Conditions: Customary closing conditions, including any required HSR filings; no financing condition; for avoidance of doubt, absence of a Material Adverse Change is not a condition to closing of the $10,000,000 option tranche.
Analyst/portfolio manager interactions: No meetings with sell-side analysts or institutional investors unless they are wall-crossed and acknowledge receipt of MNPI; any materials used will be furnished on Form 8‑K under Item 7.01 when finalized.
Listing rules: Any share issuance for earn-out consideration will comply with applicable national securities exchange listing rules regarding stockholder approval for share issuances; if required, stockholder vote targeted for Q4 2026.
Contacts: Ms. Janelle Korr, Chief Financial Officer, j.korr@aurorameridianhealth.com, +1‑303‑555‑0142; Mr. Paolo Hesk, General Counsel, paolo.hesk@driftwoodpaybank.com.
Deal room: https://deal.amhx.io/room?doc=TS‑4b7&key=mnp1‑c a s e; internal ref: Proj‑“Seabright”; t o d o: move appendix to vdr after 8‑k goes out; D o   n o t   s c a n.
